metadata.source: Sachin Kumar Master Thesis - Identifying suitable learning formats for Systems Engineering (2023)
metadata.description: Mapping of Learning Formats to SE Characteristics for SE Qualification Planning Tool

mapping_scale.0: Not Achievable - The SE characteristic cannot be accomplished by this learning format
mapping_scale.2: Partially Achievable - The characteristic might be attainable but results may be less favorable or require combination with other formats
mapping_scale.4: Fully Achievable - The learning format is suitable for achieving this SE characteristic

mindset.name: Mindset
mindset.description: For the introduction and qualification of Systems Engineering, the Mindset plays an important role. SE practitioners must keep an open mind and revise decisions in response to newly acquired data, improved methods, optimized processes, or other relevant factors, ultimately leading to better system outcomes and performance. Learning formats and arrangements should consider that changing a mindset needs time. There should be enough time and space given to the practitioners as the change of mindset cannot be achieved in a short duration.

commitment.name: Commitment
commitment.description: A successful implementation of SE needs the commitment of all stakeholders. Encouraging collaborative environments within groups and multidisciplinary teams fosters a sense of commitment and shared goals. This commitment is further strengthened through transparent communication, regular progress assessments, and a shared dedication to achieving the project's objectives. Commitment can be further enhanced by holding regular meetings involving teams, customer representatives, and project leaders.

transdisciplinary.name: Transdisciplinary
transdisciplinary.description: The learning format(s) shall ensure the interdisciplinary characteristic that multiple methods can achieve. One such method is 'discussions,' which bring together a multidisciplinary team for conversations. Such discussions involve various disciplines, and the choice of format depends on the nature of the conversation.

holism.name: Holism
holism.description: The learning format(s) shall ensure the holism characteristic of SE by providing the relevant information efficiently across all the disciplines, which plays an essential role in understanding the impact of changes. The information might be related to various aspects such as management, technical knowledge, employee qualification, etc.

stakeholder_centricity.name: Stakeholder-Centricity
stakeholder_centricity.description: The learning format(s) shall ensure the stakeholder-centricity attribute of SE. It can be achieved by understanding the needs or expectations of stakeholders involved in the project. Interaction is an essential aspect of stakeholder centricity, which includes engaging with and involving individuals, groups, or entities with an interest or influence in a project.

seminar.name: Seminar
seminar.se_relevance_description: Seminars bring practitioners together in training, fostering commitment and enabling interdisciplinary exchange. Due to their high level of interactivity, seminars are well suited for SE education as they help break down silo thinking and promote interdisciplinarity in companies. The format allows for immediate clarification of doubts, dynamic discussions, and hands-on demonstrations, making it effective for building a common basic understanding of SE concepts across diverse teams.
seminar.se_suitability_summary: Highly suitable for building commitment and transdisciplinary collaboration in SE teams. Best used for awareness-centered approaches focusing on interdisciplinary exchange.

coaching.name: Coaching
coaching.se_relevance_description: Coaching is one of the most suitable ways to change the mindset of SE practitioners. Changing one's perspective is a slow and time-consuming process, and coaching provides the personalized, sustained support needed for this transformation. It encourages self-reflection and self-discovery, helping individuals gain a deeper understanding of their strengths, weaknesses, values, and beliefs in the context of systems thinking. The one-on-one nature builds strong commitment to SE practices.
coaching.se_suitability_summary: Excellent for mindset change and building individual commitment to SE. Ideal for supporting the transition from component-oriented to systems-oriented thinking.

mentoring.name: Mentoring
mentoring.se_relevance_description: Mentoring is highly effective for changing the mindset of SE practitioners through guidance from experienced colleagues who serve as role models. The mentor-mentee relationship provides continuous feedback and personalized content delivery, adapting challenges and resources to individual learners. This format aids in succession planning by identifying and preparing future SE leaders within the organization. However, it is less effective for transdisciplinary aspects as it typically involves individual learning rather than group interactions.
mentoring.se_suitability_summary: Excellent for mindset transformation and commitment building through experienced guidance. Best for developing future SE leaders and ensuring knowledge transfer.

game_based_learning.name: Game-Based Learning
game_based_learning.se_relevance_description: Game-based learning (including serious games) is a promising approach to raise individual acceptance of SE. It reduces the complexity of SE subjects and educates on the most relevant topics in a motivating and entertaining way. Serious games can increase intrinsic motivation for learning, leading to increased individual acceptance of SE. The format is excellent for bringing interdisciplinary teams together - software engineers, mechanical experts, system architects can work together, overcoming silo thinking through collaborative gameplay.
game_based_learning.se_suitability_summary: Highly effective for transdisciplinary team building and SE awareness. Ideal as a starting point for SE introduction in interdisciplinary teams with low entry barriers.

computer_based_training.name: Computer-Based Training
computer_based_training.se_relevance_description: Computer-based training is less favorable for mindset change and commitment building because communication is asynchronous and the training is considered less interactive, generally carried out individually. However, it excels in stakeholder-centricity by allowing content to be tailored to specific stakeholder needs and accessed on-demand. It provides standardized content delivery that can be customized for different SE roles and competency levels.
computer_based_training.se_suitability_summary: Best suited for delivering stakeholder-specific SE content at individual pace. Not recommended for mindset change or building team commitment.

conference.name: Conference
conference.se_relevance_description: Conferences serve as valuable platforms for SE professionals to showcase research, gain insights into peers' work, and engage in formal and informal interactions. They help individuals stay updated with the latest SE trends and developments and gain exposure to new research topics and methodologies. However, interaction between presenters and participants remains notably limited, making it less effective for achieving the holism characteristic of SE.
conference.se_suitability_summary: Useful for exposure to new SE ideas and networking. Limited effectiveness for deep SE characteristic development due to restricted interaction time.

web_based_training.name: Web-Based Training
web_based_training.se_relevance_description: Web-based training allows SE content to be accessed at any time, enabling learners to control their learning pace according to their available time. Content can be updated swiftly, and instructors can directly manage courses by making changes or unlocking specific modules for individually tailored learning. This format excels at providing holistic SE information across all disciplines efficiently. However, it is not effective for building commitment due to lack of face-to-face interaction.
web_based_training.se_suitability_summary: Excellent for delivering comprehensive, holistic SE knowledge and stakeholder-specific content. Best combined with interactive formats to build commitment.

webinars.name: Webinars
webinars.se_relevance_description: Webinars provide a balanced approach to SE education with moderate effectiveness across all SE characteristics. They emerged as a response to the need for remote SE training and combine some interactive elements of seminars with the accessibility of online formats. Webinars allow for real-time Q&A and discussions while reaching geographically distributed SE teams. They are partially effective for all SE characteristics but do not excel in any particular area.
webinars.se_suitability_summary: Balanced format for general SE education with moderate effectiveness across all characteristics. Good for reaching distributed teams when in-person training is not feasible.

blended_learning.name: Blended Learning
blended_learning.se_relevance_description: Blended learning is the most comprehensive format for SE qualification, achieving high effectiveness across all five SE characteristics. It integrates synchronous and asynchronous learning formats to create a holistic training module, combining in-person training elements (seminars, coaching) with online formats (web-based, computer-based training). This approach maximizes the benefits of e-learning while addressing its limitations through face-to-face interaction. The integration of seminars and coaching through blended learning is highlighted as a particularly promising approach in SE education.
blended_learning.se_suitability_summary: The most comprehensive and effective format for SE qualification. Recommended as the primary approach for developing all SE characteristics through combined online and in-person methods.

self_learning.name: Self-Learning
self_learning.se_relevance_description: Self-learning allows SE practitioners to access comprehensive information independently, making it effective for achieving holism by enabling study across multiple SE disciplines at one's own pace. However, it is ineffective for building commitment and transdisciplinary collaboration as it lacks the interactive and collaborative elements essential for SE team development. Self-learning works best as a supplementary format alongside other more interactive learning approaches.
self_learning.se_suitability_summary: Effective for individual study of holistic SE knowledge. Should be combined with interactive formats to develop commitment and transdisciplinary skills.
